name: Pantera
description: Pantera is an American heavy metal band formed in Arlington, Texas in 1981 by the Abbott brothers -- guitarist Dimebag Darrell and drummer Vinnie Paul. Credited with developing and popularizing groove metal in the 1990s, Pantera sold over 20 million records worldwide and is regarded as one of the most influential bands in heavy metal history.
trivia: Far Beyond Driven debuted at #1 on the Billboard 200 in 1994 -- one of the heaviest albums ever to achieve that feat.
Dimebag Darrell was offered the lead guitar position in Megadeth but turned it down because they wouldn't also take his brother Vinnie Paul.
The band's name was inspired by the De Tomaso Pantera sports car, suggested to Vinnie Paul by a high school friend.
Pantera has sold over 20 million records worldwide and received four Grammy nominations without ever winning.
Dimebag Darrell was only 16 years old when Pantera recorded their debut album Metal Magic in 1983.
The original album cover for Far Beyond Driven featured a drill bit impaling an anus and was banned, replaced with a skull impaled by a drill.
Phil Anselmo's heart stopped for nearly 5 minutes after a heroin overdose in 1996 -- he was revived with an adrenaline shot.
